title: Match mobile UI colors to desktop and improve cross-device responsiveness

requirements.summary: Ensure all color tokens, backgrounds, text colors, accent colors, and component styles are consistently applied across all screen sizes with no visual discrepancies between breakpoints
acceptanceCriteria: Color tokens defined in frontend/src/index.css and frontend/index.css are applied uniformly at all breakpoints
Dark and light mode colors match between mobile and desktop viewports on all pages
No page has a different background, card, or text color on mobile vs desktop
Gradient, accent, and chart colors are consistent across all screen sizes

file_operations.description: Review and consolidate all color token definitions to ensure they are consistently defined for both light and dark modes without breakpoint-specific overrides. Verify that all OKLCH color values, chart colors, stress-level colors, and background tokens are properly scoped to :root and .dark selectors only. Remove any media query blocks that redefine color tokens for specific screen sizes. Ensure all custom properties are applied uniformly across all viewports.

Audit and remove any responsive color overrides or breakpoint-specific theme customizations. Ensure that all CSS custom properties for the design token system (colors, radius, chart colors, sidebar) are defined once and applied consistently at all screen sizes. Consolidate any duplicate color definitions between this file and frontend/src/index.css to maintain a single source of truth.

Review the Header component and ensure that all background colors, text colors, and border colors use the design token CSS custom properties (e.g., bg-background, text-foreground, border-border) without any breakpoint-specific color classes. Verify that the sticky positioning and color scheme remain consistent across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.

Ensure the BottomNav component uses consistent color tokens from the design system (background, foreground, accent, muted) without any responsive color overrides. Verify that active route highlighting uses the same accent color at all screen sizes. Confirm that the fixed positioning and color scheme remain uniform across all viewports.

Review the StressGauge component and ensure that the color-coded styling for low/medium/high stress levels uses the same color tokens (stress-low, stress-medium, stress-high) at all breakpoints. Verify that the glow effect and SVG colors are applied uniformly across mobile, tablet, and desktop. Remove any viewport-specific color logic.

Ensure the Recharts bar chart uses the same chart color tokens (chart-1, chart-2, chart-3, chart-4, chart-5) and stress-level colors at all screen sizes. Verify that the color-coded bars for low, medium, and high stress levels remain consistent across all viewports. Remove any breakpoint-specific fill or stroke color overrides.

Review the TipCard component and ensure that the category-specific color accents and badge colors use consistent design tokens without responsive overrides. Verify that background colors, text colors, and borders are applied uniformly across all screen sizes.

Ensure the SoundTile component uses consistent background, text, and accent colors from the design system at all breakpoints. Verify that the animated sound wave indicator and hover states use the same color tokens across mobile, tablet, and desktop. Remove any viewport-specific color classes.

Audit the Dashboard page and ensure that all background colors, card colors, text colors, gradient colors, and accent colors use the design token system consistently at all screen sizes. Verify that the live stress gauge, quick action buttons, and greeting section maintain the same color scheme across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.

Review the Breathe page and ensure that all background colors, button colors, and text colors are applied uniformly across all breakpoints. Verify that the animated breathing timer and technique selection UI maintain consistent colors at all screen sizes.

Ensure the Sounds page uses consistent background, card, and accent colors at all screen sizes. Verify that the sound tile grid and local music upload section maintain the same color scheme across mobile, tablet, and desktop.

Review the Journal page and ensure that mood selector colors, card backgrounds, text colors, and button colors are applied uniformly across all viewports. Verify that the mood entry list maintains consistent color styling at all screen sizes.

Ensure the Analytics page uses consistent colors for summary cards, chart backgrounds, text, and stress-level indicators at all breakpoints. Verify that the 7-day stress chart and improvement trend message maintain the same color scheme across mobile, tablet, and desktop.

Review the Profile page and ensure that avatar colors, background colors, card colors, and button colors are applied uniformly at all screen sizes. Verify that the profile stats section maintains consistent color styling across all viewports.

Ensure the Settings page uses consistent background, card, toggle, and button colors at all breakpoints. Verify that the theme toggle, reminder toggles, and data export button maintain the same color scheme across mobile, tablet, and desktop.

Review the SleepTracking page and ensure that form colors, input colors, button colors, quality label colors, and card backgrounds are applied uniformly across all screen sizes. Verify that the sleep entry list maintains consistent color styling at all viewports.

Ensure the VibrationControl page uses consistent background, button, slider, and text colors at all breakpoints. Verify that the pattern selection and intensity controls maintain the same color scheme across mobile, tablet, and desktop.

Review the TipsAndReminders page and ensure that tip card colors, toggle colors, and background colors are applied uniformly across all screen sizes. Verify that the wellness tips and reminder settings maintain consistent color styling at all viewports.

requirements.summary: Make the app fully responsive across all screen sizes (mobile 320px+, tablet 768px+, desktop 1024px+) with adaptive layouts, grids, charts, and navigation elements
acceptanceCriteria: No horizontal scrollbar or overflow on any page at any standard viewport width
Grid layouts (e.g., sound tiles, stats cards) reflow correctly from 1-column mobile to 2–3 column desktop
StressChart, StressGauge, and all Recharts visualizations resize fluidly without clipping
Header and BottomNav are correctly positioned and sized on mobile, tablet, and desktop
Typography scales appropriately across breakpoints using Tailwind responsive classes
All forms, sliders, and modals (e.g., ProfileSetupModal) are usable and correctly sized on all screen widths
EmergencyCalmOverlay fills the screen correctly on all device sizes

file_operations.description: Review the Layout component and ensure that the max-width container, Header, Outlet, and BottomNav are correctly positioned and sized across all screen sizes. Add responsive padding classes (e.g., px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8) to ensure proper spacing on mobile, tablet, and desktop. Verify that the scrollable main content area adapts gracefully to all viewports without overflow.

Ensure the Header component is fully responsive with proper spacing and sizing across all screen sizes. Use Tailwind responsive classes for padding, height, and font sizes (e.g., h-14 sm:h-16 lg:h-20, text-sm sm:text-base lg:text-lg). Verify that the logo, tips button, avatar, and settings button are correctly positioned and sized on mobile (320px+), tablet (768px+), and desktop (1024px+) viewports without overflow.

Review the BottomNav component and ensure it is correctly positioned and sized on mobile and tablet viewports. Add responsive padding and icon sizing classes (e.g., py-2 sm:py-3, text-base sm:text-lg). Verify that all five route links are evenly spaced and usable at all screen widths. Consider hiding the BottomNav on desktop (lg:hidden) if a sidebar or top navigation is introduced.

Ensure the StressGauge SVG component resizes fluidly across all screen sizes without clipping. Use responsive viewBox scaling and container sizing (e.g., w-full max-w-xs sm:max-w-sm lg:max-w-md mx-auto) to maintain proper proportions. Verify that text elements within the SVG scale appropriately and remain readable on mobile, tablet, and desktop.

Review the Recharts bar chart component and ensure it resizes fluidly without clipping at all viewport widths. Use responsive container sizing and ResponsiveContainer from Recharts with percentage-based dimensions. Adjust axis labels, tick fonts, and bar widths to scale appropriately on mobile (single-column), tablet (2-column), and desktop (3-column) layouts. Verify that the chart remains usable and readable at 320px+ width.

Ensure the animated breathing circle scales and positions correctly on all screen sizes. Use responsive sizing classes (e.g., w-32 h-32 sm:w-40 sm:h-40 lg:w-48 lg:h-48) to adapt the circle size across mobile, tablet, and desktop. Verify that the animation and color transitions remain smooth at all breakpoints.

Review the three-column stat grid and ensure it reflows correctly from a single column on mobile to two or three columns on tablet and desktop. Use responsive grid classes (e.g., grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4). Verify that stat cards are properly sized and spaced at all viewport widths without overflow.

Ensure the mood button row is fully responsive and usable on mobile, tablet, and desktop. Use responsive sizing and spacing classes for buttons (e.g., gap-2 sm:gap-3 lg:gap-4, text-sm sm:text-base lg:text-lg). Verify that all five mood buttons fit within the viewport at 320px+ width without requiring horizontal scrolling.

Review the SoundTile component and ensure it scales correctly within responsive grid layouts. Use responsive padding, sizing, and typography classes (e.g., p-3 sm:p-4 lg:p-6, text-sm sm:text-base lg:text-lg). Verify that the tile remains usable and visually consistent across all screen sizes.

Ensure the TipCard component is fully responsive with proper padding, typography, and layout across all screen sizes. Use responsive classes (e.g., p-4 sm:p-5 lg:p-6, text-sm sm:text-base lg:text-lg). Verify that the card adapts gracefully within single-column mobile and multi-column desktop layouts without overflow.

Review the ProfileSetupModal and ensure it is correctly sized and usable on mobile, tablet, and desktop. Use responsive modal sizing classes (e.g., max-w-sm sm:max-w-md lg:max-w-lg). Verify that the avatar selector grid and name input field are properly sized and spaced at all viewport widths. Ensure the modal content does not overflow or clip on small screens (320px+).

Ensure the EmergencyCalmOverlay fills the screen correctly on all device sizes without overflow or clipping. Use fixed positioning with full viewport dimensions (fixed inset-0 w-screen h-screen). Verify that the breathing, body scan, and affirmation phases are properly sized and readable on mobile, tablet, and desktop. Use responsive typography classes for phase instructions (e.g., text-lg sm:text-xl lg:text-2xl).

Review the IntensitySlider component and ensure it is fully responsive with proper sizing and spacing across all screen sizes. Use responsive padding, label sizing, and indicator spacing classes (e.g., px-2 sm:px-4 lg:px-6, text-sm sm:text-base lg:text-lg). Verify that the slider and dot indicators remain usable on mobile (320px+) and scale appropriately on larger screens.

Ensure the StarRating component is fully responsive with proper star sizing and spacing across all screen sizes. Use responsive icon size classes (e.g., w-6 h-6 sm:w-7 sm:h-7 lg:w-8 lg:h-8, gap-1 sm:gap-2). Verify that all five stars are visible and usable on mobile (320px+) without overflow.

Review the avatar grid and ensure it reflows correctly from a single column on mobile to multiple columns on tablet and desktop. Use responsive grid classes (e.g., grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-6 gap-2 sm:gap-3 lg:gap-4). Verify that all six avatar options are properly sized and spaced at all viewport widths.

Ensure the ReminderToggle row component is fully responsive with proper spacing and sizing across all screen sizes. Use responsive padding and typography classes (e.g., py-3 sm:py-4 lg:py-5, text-sm sm:text-base lg:text-lg). Verify that the emoji, label, and toggle switch are correctly positioned and usable on mobile, tablet, and desktop.

Review the Dashboard page and ensure all layouts, grids, and components are fully responsive. Use responsive grid and spacing classes for the greeting, stress gauge, sensor data, and quick action buttons (e.g., grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3, gap-4 sm:gap-6 lg:gap-8). Verify that the page adapts gracefully from single-column mobile (320px+) to multi-column desktop (1024px+) without overflow. Ensure typography scales appropriately (e.g., text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl for headings).

Ensure the Breathe page layout is fully responsive with proper spacing and sizing for the technique selector, breathing timer, and action buttons. Use responsive padding, grid, and typography classes (e.g., px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8, text-lg sm:text-xl lg:text-2xl). Verify that the page content fits within the viewport at all screen sizes without horizontal scrolling.

Review the Sounds page and ensure the sound tile grid reflows correctly from a single column on mobile to 2–3 columns on tablet and desktop. Use responsive grid classes (e.g., grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4 sm:gap-6). Verify that the local music upload section is properly sized and spaced at all viewport widths. Ensure no overflow or clipping occurs at 320px+ width.

Ensure the Journal page is fully responsive with proper spacing and sizing for the mood selector, note input, submit button, and mood entry list. Use responsive padding, grid, and typography classes (e.g., px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8, text-base sm:text-lg lg:text-xl). Verify that the page adapts gracefully across all screen sizes without overflow. Ensure the mood entry list cards reflow correctly on mobile, tablet, and desktop.

Review the Analytics page and ensure the summary cards grid reflows correctly from a single column on mobile to 2–3 columns on tablet and desktop. Use responsive grid classes (e.g., grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4 sm:gap-6). Verify that the 7-day stress chart resizes fluidly without clipping at all viewport widths. Ensure the improvement trend message and per-day breakdown are properly sized and spaced on mobile, tablet, and desktop.

Ensure the Profile page is fully responsive with proper spacing and sizing for the avatar, name editor, profile stats, and logout button. Use responsive padding, grid, and typography classes (e.g., px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8, text-lg sm:text-xl lg:text-2xl). Verify that the profile stats grid reflows correctly from single-column mobile to multi-column desktop. Ensure no overflow or clipping occurs at all screen sizes.

Review the Settings page and ensure the theme toggle, reminder toggles, and data export button are fully responsive. Use responsive padding, spacing, and typography classes (e.g., px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8, text-base sm:text-lg lg:text-xl). Verify that all form elements and buttons are properly sized and usable on mobile (320px+), tablet (768px+), and desktop (1024px+) without overflow.

Ensure the SleepTracking page is fully responsive with proper spacing and sizing for the time inputs, quality rating, submit button, and sleep entry list. Use responsive padding, grid, and typography classes (e.g., px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8, text-base sm:text-lg lg:text-xl). Verify that the form fields are properly sized on mobile and that the sleep entry list cards reflow correctly on tablet and desktop. Ensure no overflow or clipping occurs at all screen sizes.

Review the VibrationControl page and ensure the pattern selection, intensity slider, and submit button are fully responsive. Use responsive padding, spacing, and typography classes (e.g., px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8, text-base sm:text-lg lg:text-xl). Verify that all form elements are properly sized and usable on mobile, tablet, and desktop without overflow.

Ensure the TipsAndReminders page is fully responsive with proper spacing and sizing for the tip cards grid and reminder toggles. Use responsive grid classes for tip cards (e.g., grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4 sm:gap-6). Verify that the page adapts gracefully from single-column mobile to multi-column desktop without overflow. Ensure reminder toggle rows are properly sized and spaced at all viewport widths.

Review the Tailwind configuration and ensure that all custom breakpoints, font sizes, spacing scales, and container settings support responsive layouts across mobile (320px+), tablet (768px+), and desktop (1024px+) viewports. Verify that the typography plugin and container-queries plugin are correctly configured to support fluid scaling and responsive typography. Add or adjust responsive utility classes as needed to support the implementation of fully responsive layouts throughout the app.

Ensure the LoginScreen is fully responsive with proper padding, typography, and button sizing across all screen sizes. Use responsive classes (e.g., px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8, text-lg sm:text-xl lg:text-2xl) for the app features list and 'Get Started' button. Verify that the full-screen layout adapts gracefully on mobile, tablet, and desktop without overflow.
